description_long: This is a medieval-style helmet made of dark, metallic material, featuring a rounded dome and a pronounced ridge running from front to back. The front of the helmet has a rectangular opening for the eyes and face, with two vertical plates extending downward to protect the cheeks and jaw. Each cheek guard is perforated with a pattern of small circular holes, likely for ventilation and to reduce weight. The helmet is riveted along the lower edge and around the sides, indicating robust construction. The surface is smooth but not highly polished, showing some subtle texture and light reflection. There are no visible decorative elements, logos, or graphic designs, giving it a utilitarian and functional appearance.
description: A dark, rounded medieval helmet with cheek guards, rivets, and perforated ventilation holes, made of metal and lacking any decorative graphics.
description_view_0: Front view showing the eye opening, cheek guards with ventilation holes, and the central ridge.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the dome shape, rivets, and the profile of the cheek guard with its perforations.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth, rounded back and the central ridge, with rivets along the lower edge.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the cheek guard, ventilation holes, and the helmet's curvature.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: Multiple small circular holes arranged in a grid pattern on each cheek guard, providing ventilation on both sides of the helmet.
